nodes.label: AI as autonomous coder for unknown tasks

nodes.label: AI as strategic thinking partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as mere executor of my ideas

nodes.label: AI code quality and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: it is correct most of the time

nodes.grounding_span: having the AI write up simple elevator pitches that are fine tuned to a specific incubator be very poor

nodes.label: AI as research accelerator
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I've adapted to use AI to learn subjects and grow with it

nodes.label: Autonomy and personal control
nodes.grounding_span: I have my own plan/idea set up

nodes.label: Authentic company representation
nodes.grounding_span: staying on track with the company mission

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: There have been a lot of moments where I was satisfied with AI - especially within frontend development

nodes.label: Reluctance to use AI for writing
nodes.grounding_span: having the AI write up simple elevator pitches ... be very poor

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I don't really worry about it taking over my skillset, but more so how I can develop with it

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with limited back-and-forth
nodes.grounding_span: I analyze the AI's system design and then go on google to verify the approach

nodes.label: Strategic comparison of independent outputs
nodes.grounding_span: I prompt the AI with simply the context and have it think independently to see what it comes up with and to see if it's better than my idea
